channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: How 'Mufasa' rose with Aaron Pierre and Blue Ivy's voices along with new Lin-Manuel Miranda music
item.description: ESCONDIDO, Calif. (AP) — When Aaron Pierre was cast as Mufasa, the weight of following in the late James Earl Jones' legendary footsteps was enough to rattle any actor. But instead of letting the pressure roar too loudly, he harnessed his nerves to breathe fresh life into his young lion character.
item.dc:creator: JONATHAN LANDRUM Jr., Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: South Korean prosecutors seek to arrest ex-defense minister over martial law
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — South Korean prosecutors are seeking to formally arrest the former defense minister alleged to have colluded with President Yoon Suk Yeol in imposing martial law last week, as authorities are investigating whether their acts amount to rebellion.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM and KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Donald Trump says tariffs will not hurt Americans' pockets
item.description: Trump was unwilling to speculate whether the costs of tariffs would be passed on to the American consumer.
item.dc:creator: Libbey Dean, NewsNation

item.title: As Israel advances on a Syrian buffer zone, it sees peril and opportunity
item.description: TEL AVIV, Israel (AP) — The dramatic downfall of Syrian President Bashar Assad presents possible danger, and an opening, for neighboring Israel. After fighting wars on multiple fronts for months, Israel is now concerned that unrest in Syria could spill over into its territory.
item.dc:creator: MELANIE LIDMAN, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: New York prosecutors charge suspect in UnitedHealthcare CEO killing with murder, court records show
item.description: ALTOONA, Pa. (AP) — Authorities arrested a suspect and charged him with murder Monday in the brazen Manhattan killing of UnitedHealthcare’s CEO after a quick-thinking McDonald’s customer in Pennsylvania spotted a man who officers found with a gun, mask and writings linking him to the ambush.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL R. SISAK, MARK SCOLFORO and CEDAR ATTANASIO, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Video shows thieves speed off in sports cars from Michigan dealership
item.description: A group of suspected car thieves sped away from a Michigan dealership in rare sports vehicles, leading police on a high-speed chase that ended with the arrest of a 19-year-old Detroit woman.
item.dc:creator: Duncan MacLean, KREX 5

item.title: Biden's approval of an oil lease sale will keep the door open to drilling in Alaska's Arctic refuge
item.description: The Biden administration has given approval for the sale of oil and gas leases in Alaska, leaving the door open for drilling in a portion of the pristine Arctic National Wildlife Refuge.
item.dc:creator: Becky Bohrer, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Polygamous sect leader gets 50 years in prison in scheme to orchestrate sex involving children
item.description: PHOENIX (AP) — A polygamist religious leader who claimed more than 20 spiritual “wives” including 10 underage girls was sentenced to 50 years in prison on Monday for coercing girls as young as 9 years old to submit to criminal sex acts with him and other adults, and for scheming to kidnap them from
item.dc:creator: JACQUES BILLEAUD and ANITA SNOW, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Who is Luigi Mangione? Police arrest person of interest in CEO killing
item.description: A man arrested in connection with the fatal shooting of UnitedHealthcare CEO Brian Thompson has been identified as Luigi Mangione, officials said Monday.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Cook, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump voices support for RFK Jr's vaccine views, suggests Kennedy will research debunked autism link
item.description: “When you look at some of the problems, when you look at what’s going on with disease and sickness in our country, something’s wrong,” Trump said on "Meet The Press.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Weixel, NewsNation

item.title: Judge dismisses charges against Karen Read supporter who scattered rubber ducks and fake $100 bills
item.description: BOSTON (AP) — A Massachusetts judge dismissed criminal charges Monday against a backer of Karen Read who admitted placing dozens of yellow rubber ducks and fake $100 bills around town in support of Read. Richard Schiffer Jr.
item.dc:creator: STEVE LeBLANC, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Amazon offering $25 flights for Prime members — but only for one eligible group
item.description: The deal, which runs through Friday, aims to offer 1,000 bookings per day for a total of 5,000 $25 fares.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: TikTok asks federal appeals court to bar enforcement of potential ban until Supreme Court review
item.description: TikTok asked a federal appeals court on Monday to bar the Biden administration from enforcing a law that could lead to a ban on the popular platform until the Supreme Court reviews its challenge to the statute.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Oldest known stone tablet inscribed with Ten Commandments to be auctioned
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The oldest known stone tablet inscribed with the Ten Commandments, dating from 300 to 800 A.D., will be sold in New York this month, Sotheby's auction house said. The 155-pound (52-kilogram) marble slab inscribed with the commandments in Paleo-Hebrew script will be auctioned on Dec.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Suspect in UnitedHealthcare CEO killing charged with murder, court records show
item.description: A man in Pennsylvania has been arrested in connection with the fatal shooting of UnitedHealthcare CEO Brian Thompson, officials said Monday.
item.dc:creator: Erin Pflaumer, KTLA 5

item.title: Veteran Daniel Penny found not guilty in NYC subway chokehold case over Jordan Neely's death
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A Marine veteran who used a chokehold on an agitated subway rider was acquitted on Monday in a death that became a prism for differing views about public safety, valor and vigilantism.
item.dc:creator: JENNIFER PELTZ, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Tren de Aragua fugitive arrested in Tennessee
item.description: Tren de Aragua member Luis Alejandro Ruiz Godoy was arrested on a litany of charges including terrorism and human trafficking, per Interpol.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: Lara Trump stepping down from RNC amid Senate speculation
item.description: Lara Trump is stepping down as co-chair of the Republican National Committee (RNC) as she considers replacing Florida Sen. Marco Rubio (R), whom her father-in-law, President-elect Donald Trump, picked to be secretary of State.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, WGN 9

item.title: Taylor Swift’s Eras Tour ends by shattering own record, grossing an estimated $2.2B, Pollstar says
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Taylor Swift’s Eras Tour brought in $2.2 billion in its nearly two-year run, making it the highest-grossing tour of all time for a second year in a row, Pollstar estimates from data collected across 149 shows.
item.dc:creator: MARIA SHERMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Daniel Penny jury returns after judge's controversial dismissal of deadlocked top charge
item.description: New York Marine veteran Daniel Penny isn't out of the woods yet, as jurors controversially have been instructed to weigh a lesser charge for death of Jordan Neely.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Cracker Barrel 'refused service' to students with disabilities, Maryland school says
item.description: Cracker Barrel has issued an apology after a Maryland school district said a group of students with disabilities was denied service at one of the chain's restaurants.
item.dc:creator: Skyler Sales, WGN 9

item.title: 10 hospitalized after officer crashes into crowd during Palm Springs holiday parade
item.description: The officer, who was reportedly performing a wheelie trick, suddenly lost control of his bike and fell off, witnesses told the Desert Sun.
item.dc:creator: Vivian Chow, NewsNation

item.title: Saints QB Derek Carr injures left hand on dive in 4th quarter of win over Giants
item.description: New Orleans Saints quarterback Derek Carr injured his left hand late in the fourth quarter of Sunday's 14-11 victory over the New York Giants when he went airborne while trying for a first down and crashed to the turf.
item.dc:creator: TOM CANAVAN, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Trump says he can't guarantee tariffs won't raise US prices and won't rule out revenge prosecutions
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Donald Trump said he can't guarantee that his promised tariffs on key U.S. foreign trade partners won't raise prices for American consumers and he suggested once more that some political rivals and federal officials who pursued legal cases against him should be imprisoned.
item.dc:creator: BILL BARROW and WILL WEISSERT, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Woman, 52, accused of robbing Billie Eilish fans at knifepoint near Oregon arena
item.description: A woman was arrested for allegedly stabbing a man, then threatening and robbing a couple of their sleeping bag near the Moda Center Saturday morning.
item.dc:creator: Tim Steele, KRON4

item.title: 5 takeaways from Trump's 'Meet the Press' interview
item.description: President-elect Trump's first sit-down interview since winning a second White House term aired Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, KRON4

item.title: A timeline of the fatal shooting of UnitedHealthcare CEO Brian Thompson and search for his killer
item.description: After days of searching, police have announced the arrest of a suspect in the killing of UnitedHealthcare’s CEO outside a Midtown Manhattan hotel last week.
item.dc:creator: Michael R. Sisak And Cedar Attanasio, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: These states have the highest — and lowest — median salary: new data
item.description: New data released by a payroll site found median annual salaries from $40,000 to over six figures across the country.
item.dc:creator: Maddie Rhodes, ABC 4

item.title: New photos released of suspect in UnitedHealthcare CEO killing
item.description: The manhunt continues for UnitedHealthcare CEO Brian Thompson’s killer as police released new photos of the suspect.
item.dc:creator: Finn Hoogensen, ABC 4

item.title: US journalist Austin Tice, who disappeared in Syria in 2012, believed to be alive: Biden
item.description: “We think we can get him back," Biden told reporters at the White House, while acknowledging that “we have no direct evidence” of his status.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Check your junk mail: Some affected by the Equifax data breach to get more money
item.description: Customers affected by Equifax credit monitoring agency’s 2017 data breach may be eligible for another payment.
item.dc:creator: Katie Millard, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Syrian government falls in stunning end to 50-year rule of Assad family
item.description: BEIRUT (AP) — The Syrian government fell early Sunday in a stunning end to the 50-year rule of the Assad family after a sudden rebel offensive sprinted across government-held territory and entered the capital in 10 days.
item.dc:creator: BASSEM MROUE and ZEINA KARAM, Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Yes, Chipotle is raising menu prices again: Here's why
item.description: The cost of your burrito bowl is going up. Chipotle has raised menu prices for the first time in over a year.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Jackson, KTLA 5

item.title: LA Galaxy strike early, hold off New York Red Bulls 2-1 to win their record 6th MLS Cup championship
item.description: CARSON, Calif. (AP) — The LA Galaxy finished 26th in the 29-team Major League Soccer standings just one season ago, and their biggest supporters boycotted certain matches to protest a decade of poor performance.
item.dc:creator: GREG BEACHAM, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: WATCH: Doorbell camera captures Illinois garbage truck explosion
item.description: Authorities described the explosion as "significant" and said truck parts were launched into the air, landing several blocks away.
item.dc:creator: Winnie Dortch, KRON4

item.title: 2 Pearl Harbor survivors, ages 104 and 102, return to Hawaii to honor those killed in 1941 attack
item.description: The Dec. 7, 1941, bombing killed more than 2,300 U.S. servicemen. Nearly half, or 1,177, were sailors and Marines on board the USS Arizona, which sank during the battle. The remains of more than 900 Arizona crew members are still entombed on the submerged vessel.
item.dc:creator: AUDREY McAVOY, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: What happens when 'The Simpsons' join 'Monday Night Football'? Find out during Bengals-Cowboys
item.description: On Monday night, the entire Simpsons universe gets to experience it in a way not many could have imagined. The prime-time matchup between the Cincinnati Bengals and Dallas Cowboys will also take place at Springfield’s Atoms Stadium as part of “The Simpsons Funday Football” alternate broadcast.
item.dc:creator: JOE REEDY, Associated Press, Fox 5
